title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: metadata parquet files dataset can downloaded Zenodo GitHub. simplify DeconvolutionLabel1 column use input cell type labels. contains cell types 8-μ\mum binned data predicted deconvolution using single-cell RNA-seq reference. input data frame SCOPE contain following columns: CellID: cell id ImageID: sample id X: X coordinate Y: Y coordinate Celltype: cell type labels remove bins assigned “doublets” “rejected” deconvolution algorithm focus singlet bins.
code: library(arrow) # for loading parquet files  CRC_VisiumHD_metadata <- sapply(c("P1", "P2", "P5"), function(x) {   read_parquet(paste0("https://github.com/10XGenomics/HumanColonCancer_VisiumHD/",                       "raw/refs/heads/main/MetaData/", x, "CRC_Metadata.parquet"))   }, simplify = FALSE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) %>%   data.table::rbindlist(idcol = "ImageID") %>%   # Make cell ids unique   mutate(barcode = paste(barcode, ImageID, sep = "_")) %>%   # Simplifying cell type annotations   mutate(Celltype = case_when(     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Mature B", "Memory B") ~ "B cell",       DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Proliferating Macrophages",                                 "Proliferating Immune II") ~ "Prolif_Immune",     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Fibroblast", "Vascular Fibroblast",                                 "Proliferating Fibroblast") ~ "Fibroblast",      DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Enteric Glial", "Enterocyte", "Neuroendocrine") ~ "Neuronal",      str_detect(DeconvolutionLabel1, "Tumor") ~ "Tumor",     DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Epithelial", "Tuft", "Goblet") ~ "Epithelial",      DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Smooth Muscle", "SM Stress Response", "vSM") ~ "Smooth Muscle",       DeconvolutionLabel1 %in% c("Unknown III (SM)", "Adipocyte") ~ "Other",     TRUE ~ DeconvolutionLabel1   )) CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in <- CRC_VisiumHD_metadata %>%   # Keeping only the singlet bins   filter(DeconvolutionClass == "singlet") %>%   select(CellID = barcode, ImageID, X = X, Y = Y, Celltype = Celltype)  # save(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_dat_in.RData")) head(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in) # 810,330 bins #>                      CellID ImageID        X         Y Celltype #>                      <char>  <char>    <num>     <num>   <char> #> 1: s_008um_00000_00289-1_P1      P1 69.20113 -309.4240    Tumor #> 2: s_008um_00000_00290-1_P1      P1 69.44765 -309.4263    Tumor #> 3: s_008um_00000_00291-1_P1      P1 69.69418 -309.4286    Tumor #> 4: s_008um_00000_00292-1_P1      P1 69.94070 -309.4309    Tumor #> 5: s_008um_00000_00293-1_P1      P1 70.18723 -309.4332    Tumor #> 6: s_008um_00000_00294-1_P1      P1 70.43375 -309.4354    Tumor CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = celltype_colors,                       breaks = names(celltype_colors)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank())

title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: Next, use k-nearest neighbors algorithm define spatial neighborhood compute neighborhood cell type composition matrix. default number nearest neighbors kk 20, however can increase kk larger tissue slices. set kk=50. addition, enforce distance boundary nearest neighbors exclude cells falling outside radius. boundary initially defined 99th percentile distances focal cells nearest neighbors. falls outside predefined range, reset either minimum (min.radius) maximum threshold (max.radius). can examine distribution 99th percentile distance nearest neighbors select threshold parameters min.radius max.radius accordingly. 99th percentiles 2.466, 1.679, 1.939 P1, P2, P5 respectively, percentiles remain nearly identical across three samples. Therefore set max.radius 2 min.radius 1.  Cells retain fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary neighborhood cell-type proportions set NaN excluded subsequent niche clustering. set min.n.neighbors 10.
code: slide_list <- unique(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in$ImageID)  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist <- pbmcapply::pbmclapply(   slide_list, function(s) {     Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(       dat_slide=subset(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, ImageID == s),       knn_num = 50, cell_types_available = unique(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in$Celltype),       min.radius = 1,       # lower limit for the boundary       max.radius = 2,       # upper limit for the boundary       min.n.neighbors = 15, # minimum no. of neighbors       prob.list = seq(0, 1, 0.1))},   mc.cores = 3) # set mc.cores to 1 on Windows # save(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist,  #      file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_results.RData")) length(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist) #> [1] 3 sapply(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "dist.quantiles") %>%   as.data.frame() %>%    rownames_to_column("perc") %>%   mutate(perc = as.numeric(str_remove(perc, "%"))) %>%   pivot_longer(2:ncol(.), names_to = "ImageID", values_to = "value") %>%   ggplot(aes(x = perc, y = value)) +   geom_point(aes(color = ImageID)) +   scale_color_discrete(labels = slide_list) +   scale_x_continuous(labels = function(x) {paste0(x, "%")}) +   labs(y = "Distance to\nNearest Neighbors", x = "Percentile") +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) # Combine neighborhood proportion table from the three samples cell_neighbor_table_maxdist <- lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, "[[", "prop.df") %>%   do.call("rbind", .) cells_to_exclude = apply(cell_neighbor_table_maxdist, 1, function(x) {any(is.na(x))}) %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   `colnames<-`("is_na") %>%   rownames_to_column("CellID") %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in[, c("CellID", "X", "Y", "ImageID")], by = "CellID")  # Number of cells to exclude table(cells_to_exclude$is_na) #>  #>  FALSE   TRUE  #> 809156   1174  cells_to_exclude %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To\nExclude") +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank())

what: 3. Cell type triad niche (CTN) detection using SCOPE
title: Detecting lymphocyte aggregate-like niches in CRC VisiumHD data
text: Next, apply SCOPE B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} triad using default configurations following parameters: min.prop: minimum neighborhood cell-type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0 (default: 0.3). mgcv_df: dimension basis used thin-plate regression spline smoothing core cell-type proportions (default: 15; see mgcv::s() details). set number clusters clusternum 3 separate B cell T cell zones. can see B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} CTNs closely aligns lymphocyte aggregates CRC samples, CTN1 resembles B cell zone CTN2 resembles surrounding T cell zone.
code: core_celltypes <- c("B cell", "CD4 T cell", "CD8 T cell")  CTN_df <- run_SCOPE(   combination_table = as.data.frame(matrix(core_celltypes, nrow = 1)),   cell_neighbor_table = cell_neighbor_table_maxdist,   dat_in = CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in,   save_results = FALSE,    clusternum = 3, min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE, mgcv_df = 15, iter.max = 100) # save(CTN_df, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_B_CD4T_CD8T.RData")) # Number of cells per CTN table(CTN_df$label) #>  #>       CTN1       CTN2 Unassigned  #>       9121       4242     795793 # CTN label with colored dots separating the cell types CTN_label <- color_CTN_names(   paste(core_celltypes, collapse = "_"), celltype_colors)   # Visualizing B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell CTN CTN_df %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = label)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("CTN1" = "#E69F00", "CTN2" = "#56B4E9",                                  "Unassigned" = "grey90"), name = NULL) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank())  # Show core cell types only CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in %>%   mutate(Celltype = ifelse(Celltype %in% core_celltypes, Celltype, "Other")) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = celltype_colors,                       breaks = names(celltype_colors)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = "core cell type triad") +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank()) CTN_df %>%   left_join(CRC_VisiumHD_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   count(Celltype, label) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = n, y = label, fill = factor(Celltype, names(celltype_colors)))) +   geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = TRUE)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = celltype_colors, name = NULL) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 3)) +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Proportion of Cells", y = NULL) +   theme(plot.title = element_markdown(hjust = 0.5),         legend.key.size = unit(3, "mm"),         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.location = "plot",         legend.justification = "right") sessionInfo() #> R version 4.4.1 code: library(SingleCellExperiment) NSCLC_obj <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "sce_all_annotated.rds")) # Extract cell metadata NSCLC_IMC_dat_in = colData(NSCLC_obj)[,c("Tma_ac","CellID","cell_type","Center_X","Center_Y")] NSCLC_IMC_dat_in <- as.data.frame(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in)  # Rename the columns colnames(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in) <- c("ImageID","CellID","Celltype","X","Y") NSCLC_IMC_dat_in$Celltype <- factor(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in$Celltype, NSCLC_IMC_celltype_levs) ncell_by_slide <- table(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in$ImageID) slide_list = unique(names(ncell_by_slide)[which(ncell_by_slide>500)]) # 1984 images left NSCLC_IMC_dat_in <- NSCLC_IMC_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% slide_list) save(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_dat_in.RData"))

Cells retain fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary neighborhood cell-type proportions set NaN excluded subsequent niche clustering. set min.n.neighbors 10.
code: cell_neighbor_results_maxdist = pbmcapply::pbmclapply(   1:length(slide_list), function(i) {     Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(       dat_slide=dat_in[dat_in$ImageID==slide_list[i], ], knn_num = what: 3. Prefilter cell type triads using CLQ permutation test
title: Systematic CTN screening in NSCLC IMC data
text: SCOPE identifies niches characterized cell-type triads. However, number cell types grows, combinations three cell types can increase exponentially. Therefore, utilize colocation quotient (CLQ) permutation test prioritize cell-type triads (CTs) exhibit significant spatial proximity. Please refer Prefiltering cell type triads using CLQ permutation test vignette details. Note CLQ permutation test can time consuming. load precomputed results use Benjamini-Hochberg (BH) adjusted p-values prioritize cell type triads constituent pairs exhibit significant CLQs (FDR < 0.05) least 40 tissue sections.
code: load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_counts.RData")) load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_ids.RData"))  # Compute observed CLQ CLQ_obs <- compute_CLQ_observed(   cell_neighbor_counts = cell_neighbor_counts,   dat_in = NSCLC_IMC_dat_in)  # Compute permutated CLQ by randomly shuffling cell type labels for 500 times CLQ_perm <- compute_CLQ_permutated(   cell_neighbor_ids = cell_neighbor_ids,    dat_in = NSCLC_IMC_dat_in,   iter.num = 500, seed = 42, n_cores = 5)  # Compute permutation test p-value CLQ_perm_pval <- CLQ_permutation_test(CLQ_obs = CLQ_obs, CLQ_perm = CLQ_perm) load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_CLQ_perm_pval_500iter.RData")) head(CLQ_perm_pval, 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #> # Groups:   Celltype, Celltype_neighbor [1] #>   Celltype Celltype_neighbor ImageID  p_perm p_perm.adj #>   <chr>    <chr>             <chr>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 _cell    _lood             175A_1    0         0      #> 2 _cell    _lood             175A_10   1         1      #> 3 _cell    _lood             175A_100  0.002     0.0281 #> 4 _cell    _lood             175A_102  0         0      #> 5 _cell    _lood             175A_103  0.422     1 # Exclude scarce (< 500 cells in total) & uninformative cell types ("Other")  ncell_by_celltype <- table(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in$Celltype) celltype_to_exclude <- c(names(which(ncell_by_celltype < 500)), "Other") print(paste("Cell type(s) to exclude:", celltype_to_exclude)) #> [1] "Cell type(s) to exclude: Other"  # All combinations of the rest of the cell types celltype_combs <- combn(setdiff(NSCLC_IMC_celltype_levs,                                 celltype_to_exclude), 3) %>%   t() %>% as.data.frame()  # Match CLQ permutation test p-values celltype_combs_w_pval <- celltype_combs %>%   left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V1" = "Celltype", "V2" = "Celltype_neighbor"),              relationship = "many-to-many") %>%   left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V1" = "Celltype_neighbor", "V3" = "Celltype", "ImageID"),             suffix = c(".11", "")) %>%   left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V1" = "Celltype", "V3" = "Celltype_neighbor", "ImageID"),             suffix = c(".12", "")) %>%   left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V1" = "Celltype_neighbor", "V3" = "Celltype", "ImageID"),             suffix = c(".21", "")) %>%   left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V2" = "Celltype", "V3" = "Celltype_neighbor", "ImageID"),             suffix = c(".22", ""))  %>%   left_join(CLQ_perm_pval, by = c("V2" = "Celltype_neighbor", "V3" = "Celltype", "ImageID"),             suffix = c(".31", ".32"))   # Filter CTs where cell type triads where all constituent pairs exhibit significant CLQs (FDR < 0.05) combination_table_filtered = celltype_combs_w_pval %>% ungroup %>%   filter((p_perm.adj.11 < 0.05 | p_perm.adj.12 < 0.05) &           (p_perm.adj.21 < 0.05 | p_perm.adj.22 < 0.05) &           (p_perm.adj.31 < 0.05 | p_perm.adj.32 < 0.05)) %>%   group_by(V1, V2, V3) %>%   summarise(n = n()) %>%   filter(n > 40) %>%   select(-n)  print(paste("No of remaining CTs:", nrow(combination_table_filtered))) #> [1] "No of remaining CTs: 0"  # save(combination_table_filtered,  #     file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_combination_table_filtered.RData")) celltype_to_exclude <- cell_neighbor_table_maxdist[, unique(unlist(combination_table_filtered))] %>%   apply(2, function(x) {sum(x > 0.3, na.rm = TRUE)}) %>% sort() %>%   as.data.frame %>%   `colnames<-`("n") %>%   filter(n <= 50) %>%   rownames()  if(length(celltype_to_exclude) > 1) {   comb_to_run <- combination_table_filtered %>%     mutate(combs = paste(V1, V2, V3, sep = "_")) %>%     filter(!str_detect(combs, paste(celltype_to_exclude, collapse = "|"))) %>%     select(-combs) } else {   comb_to_run <- combination_table_filtered }

what: 4. Cell type triad niche (CTN) detection using SCOPE
title: Systematic CTN screening in NSCLC IMC data
text: Next, apply SCOPE systematic screening CTNs using default configurations following parameters: min.prop: minimum neighborhood cell-type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0 (default: 0.3). mgcv_df: dimension basis used thin-plate regression spline smoothing driver cell-type proportions (default: 15; see mgcv::s() details). set number clusters clusternum 2. setting save_results = TRUE, clustering results CT saved individual .RData files output_dir. gather outputs save one data frame.
code: # Load prefiltered CTs load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_combination_table_filtered.RData"))  celltype_to_exclude <- cell_neighbor_table_maxdist[, unique(unlist(combination_table_filtered))] %>%   apply(2, function(x) {sum(x > 0.3, na.rm = TRUE)}) %>% sort() %>%   as.data.frame %>%   `colnames<-`("n") %>%   filter(n <= 50) %>%   rownames()   run_SCOPE(   combination_table = combination_table_filtered,   cell_neighbor_table = cell_neighbor_table_maxdist,   dat_in = NSCLC_IMC_dat_in,   save_results = TRUE,   output_dir = paste0(data_path, "CTN_K_2"),   clusternum = 2, min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE, mgcv_df = 15, iter.max = 100) Full_CTN_table_mgcv <- data.frame(dir = list.files(   paste0(data_path, "_CTN_K_2"), ".RData", full.names = TRUE)) %>%   mutate(group = row_number() %/% 30) %>%   split(f = .$group) %>%   lapply(pull, dir) %>%   lapply(function(dirs) {     pbmclapply(dirs, function(dir) {       load(dir)       return(CTN_df)       CTN_df %>% select(CellID, CTN, label) %>%         mutate(CTN = str_replace_all(CTN, "_CAF", ".CAF")) %>%         mutate(CTN = str_replace_all(CTN, "hypoxic_tpCAF", "hypoxic.tpCAF"))     }, mc.cores = 10) %>%       do.call(rbind, .)  %>% `rownames<-`(NULL) %>%       pivot_wider(names_from = "comb", values_from = "label") })  Full_CTN_table_mgcv <- lapply(Full_CTN_table_mgcv, function(df) {df %>% select(-CellID)}) %>%   `names<-`(NULL) %>%   do.call(cbind, .) %>%   cbind(data.frame(CellID = Full_CTN_table_mgcv[[1]]$CellID), .)  Full_CTN_table_mgcv <- temp %>%   left_join(dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   mutate(Celltype = as.character(Celltype)) %>%   mutate(Celltype = str_replace(as.character(Celltype), "_CAF", ".CAF")) %>%   mutate(Celltype = str_replace(Celltype, "hypoxic_tpCAF", "hypoxic.tpCAF"))    select(all_of(colnames(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in)), everything()) %>%    save(Full_CTN_table_mgcv, file = paste0(   data_path, dataset_name, "_Full_CTN_table_mgcv.RData")) rm(temp)

title: Systematic CTN screening in NSCLC IMC data
text: Sometimes CTNs driven two different triads can highly similar. merge highly overlapping CTNs, calculate Jaccard distance every pair CTNs perform hierarchical clustering reduce redundancy. Silhouette scores utilized determine optimal number clusters. final cluster count set 77, balancing mean median Silhouette scores.  heatmap visualization Jaccard similarity indices (1-Jaccard index) hierarchical clustering results. Black boxes outline final CTN groupings. Asterisks (*) dots (.) indicate highly similar (Jaccard index > 0.95) moderately similar (0.9 ≤\leq Jaccard index ≤\leq 0.95) CTN pairs, respectively.  merging highly overlapping initial CTNs using hierarchical clustering, next step assign new names consolidated niches. package uses CTN_annotate() function rename consolidated CTNs based frequent core cell types within original triads. tie occurs cell types, function automatically resolves identifying cell type highest overall proportion within consolidated CTN. First, compute cell type proportion within newly consolidated CTNs, used one inputs CTN_annotate() function. Additional inputs include data frame containing original CTN names hierarchical cluster labels, character vector defining levels ordering cell types. CTN_annotate function returns list two data frames: annotation: mapping data frame matches original CTN names hierarchical cluster labels final consolidated names core_celltypes_summ: summary table focusing clusters contain least 2 initial CTNs. displays many times core cell type appeared original CTN names tracks relative proportions within merged CTN. top two frequent cell types appear twice original CTN names, function flags consolidated CTN asterisk (*). suggest double-checking flagged CTNs manually renaming necessary. also manually renamed CTNs high blood (“Blood”), lymphatic endothelial (“Lymphatic”), vCAF proportions “Vessel”. can perform hierarchical clustering Jaccard distance consolidated CTNs grouping major categories. categories can annotated examining cell type abundance constituent CTNs.
code: load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_Full_CTN_table_mgcv.RData"))  # All cell type triads CTN_list <- setdiff(colnames(Full_CTN_table_mgcv), colnames(NSCLC_IMC_dat_in))  CTN_jaccard_mat_hclust title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: rds file containing Seurat object dataset can downloaded Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) accesssion number GSE250346. downloaded GSE250346_Seurat_GSE250346_CORRECTED_SEE_RDS_README_082024.rds file saved <data_path>/. also use cells_partitioned_by_annotation.csv file GSE250346_HE_annotations.tar.gz contains annotations PF histopathological features (including TLSs). samples file need renamed following code original paper: input data frame SCOPE contain following columns: CellID: cell id ImageID: sample id X: X coordinate Y: Y coordinate Celltype: cell type labels four samples containing TLSs:
code: library(Seurat)  PF_Xenium_obj <- readRDS(paste0(data_path, "GSE250346_Seurat_GSE250346_CORRECTED_SEE_RDS_README_082024.rds")) clinical_data <- distinct(PF_Xenium_obj@meta.data, sample, sample_affect)  # save(clinical_data, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_clinical_data.RData"))) load(paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_clinical_data.RData")) PF_Xenium_annot <- read.csv(paste0(data_path, "cells_partitioned_by_annotation.csv")) %>%   ## keeping only the relevant columns to reduce data size   select(CellID = cell_id, ImageID = sample, Annotation_Type, annotation_type_instance) %>%   mutate(patient = str_extract(ImageID, "(THD|TILD|VUHD|VUILD)[0-9]+")) %>%   left_join(clinical_data[, c("patient", "sample_affect")], by = "patient") %>%   ## Change sample name based on the code from the original paper (   ## Initial rename   mutate(ImageID_new = case_when(     sample_affect == "Less Affected" ~ paste0(patient, "LA"),     sample_affect == "More Affected"  ~ paste0(patient, "MA"),     TRUE ~ ImageID)) %>%   ## Fix additional names   mutate(ImageID_new = case_when(     ImageID == "TILD117MF" ~ "TILD117MA1",     ImageID == "TILD117MFB" ~ "TILD117MA2",     ImageID == "VUILD104LF" ~ "VUILD104MA1",     ImageID == "VUILD104MF" ~ "VUILD104MA2",     ImageID == "VUILD105LF" ~ "VUILD105MA1",     ImageID == "VUILD105MF" ~ "VUILD105MA2",     ImageID == "VUILD48LF" ~ "VUILD48LA1",     ImageID == "VUILD48MF" ~ "VUILD48LA2",     str_detect(ImageID_new, 'LF$') ~ str_replace(ImageID_new, "LF$", "LA"),     str_detect(ImageID_new, 'MF$') ~ str_replace(ImageID_new, "MF$", "MA"),     TRUE ~ ImageID_new)) %>%   mutate(CellID = paste(ImageID_new, CellID, sep = "_")) %>%   select(-ImageID) %>%   rename(ImageID = ImageID_new)  # save(PF_Xenium_annot, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_annotations.RData")) PF_Xenium_dat_in <- PF_Xenium_obj@meta.data %>%   select(CellID = full_cell_id, ImageID = sample,           X = x_centroid, Y = y_centroid, Celltype = final_CT) ## save(PF_Xenium_dat_in, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_dat_in.RData")) TLS_img_list <- unique(subset(PF_Xenium_annot, Annotation_Type == "TLS")$ImageID)  ## Images containing TLSs, colored by cell type PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% c(TLS_img_list)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette,                       breaks = names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom")  ## Images containing TLSs, colored by annotations PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_annot[, c("CellID", "Annotation_Type")], by = "CellID") %>%   filter(ImageID %in% c(TLS_img_list)) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Annotation_Type)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(breaks = names(annotation_palette), na.value = "grey90",                      values = annotation_palette, name = NULL) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3))) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.5, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom")

title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: Next, use k-nearest neighbors algorithm define spatial neighborhood compute neighborhood cell type composition matrix. default number nearest neighbors kk 20, however can increase kk larger tissues. Since dataset contains 3-5mm lung tissue cores, set k=50k=50. addition, enforce distance boundary nearest neighbors exclude cells falling outside radius. boundary initially defined 99th percentile distances focal cells nearest neighbors. falls outside predefined range, reset either minimum (min.radius) maximum threshold (max.radius). can examine distribution 99th percentile distance nearest neighbors select threshold parameters min.radius max.radius accordingly. set max.radius min.radius default values (500 100 respectively).  can see two outlier samples represent sparsely populated regions tissue.  cells retaining fewer min.n.neighbors neighbors within distance boundary, neighborhood cell-type proportions set NaN excluded subsequent niche clustering. set min.n.neighbors 10.
code:
slide_list <- unique(PF_Xenium_dat_in$ImageID)  cell_neighbor_results_maxdist <- pbmcapply::pbmclapply(   slide_list, function(s) {     Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(       dat_slide=subset(PF_Xenium_dat_in, ImageID == s),       knn_num = 50, cell_types_available = unique(PF_Xenium_dat_in$Celltype),       min.radius = 100,       # lower limit for the boundary       max.radius = 500,       # upper limit for the boundary       min.n.neighbors = 10,   # minimum no. of neighbors,       return.nn.ids = TRUE,   # Whether to return nearest neighbor Cell IDs       prob.list = seq(0, 1, 0.1))},   mc.cores = 4) # set mc.cores=1 on Windows machine  # Extract neighborhood cell count and ids and save it into a separate file cell_neighbor_counts_nnids <-  lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, function(x) {x[c("count.df", "neighbor.cell.ids")]}) # save(cell_neighbor_counts_nnids, #      file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_counts_nnids.RData")) # Extract neighborhood cell type proportion and  # quantiles of distance to nearest neighbors cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles <-  lapply(   cell_neighbor_results_maxdist, function(x) {x[c("prop.df", "dist.quantiles")]}) # save(cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles,  #      file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles.RData"))  rm(cell_neighbor_results_maxdist) ## 99th percentile of the distance to nearest neighbors dist_99th <- sapply(cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles, "[[", "dist.quantiles") %>%   `colnames<-`(slide_list) %>%   as.data.frame() %>% rownames_to_column("perc") %>%   mutate(perc = as.numeric(str_remove(perc, "%"))) %>%   pivot_longer(2:ncol(.), names_to = "ImageID", values_to = "value") %>%   filter(perc == 99)  ## Distribution of 99th percentile dist_99th %>%   ggplot(aes(x = value)) +   geom_histogram(color = "white", fill = "skyblue", bins = 20) +   geom_boxplot() +   geom_text(data = subset(dist_99th, value > 150),              aes(label = ImageID, y = 2),              hjust = 0, size = 3.5, angle = 90) +   labs(y = "99th percentile of Dist.
to Nearest Neighbors", x = NULL) +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, angle = 45)) img_outliers <- arrange(subset(dist_99th, value > 150), value)$ImageID PF_Xenium_dat_in %>%   filter(ImageID %in% img_outliers) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette,                       breaks = names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette),                      labels = rename_celltype, name = NULL) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 5)) +   facet_wrap(~ factor(ImageID, img_outliers), scales = "free") +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         panel.background = element_blank(),         aspect.ratio = 1,         legend.key.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "mm"),         legend.position = "bottom") # Combine neighborhood proportion table cell_neighbor_table_maxdist <- lapply(   cell_neighbor_props_dist.quantiles, "[[", "prop.df") %>%   do.call("rbind", .) cells_to_exclude = apply(cell_neighbor_table_maxdist, 1, function(x) {any(is.na(x))}) %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   `colnames<-`("is_na") %>%   rownames_to_column("CellID") %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in[, c("CellID", "X", "Y", "ImageID")], by = "CellID")  # Number of cells to exclude table(cells_to_exclude$is_na) #>  #>   FALSE    TRUE  #> 1628906    1413  # Cells to be removed from the two sparse samples cells_to_exclude %>%   filter(ImageID %in% img_outliers) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na, size = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To
Exclude") +   scale_size_manual(values = c(0.1, 1)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1), size = "none") +   facet_wrap(~factor(ImageID, img_outliers), scales = "free") +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         aspect.ratio = 1,         panel.background = element_blank()) # Cells to be removed from the samples containing TLSs cells_to_exclude %>%   filter(ImageID %in% TLS_img_list) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = is_na, size = is_na)) +   geom_point(shape = 16) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("brown2", "grey90"),                       breaks = c(TRUE, FALSE), name = "Cells To
Exclude") +   scale_size_manual(values = c(0.1, 0.5)) +   guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1), size = "none") +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void() +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         aspect.ratio = 1,         panel.background = element_blank())

what: 3. Cell type triad niche (CTN) detection using SCOPE
title: Detecting TLS-like CTNs in PF Xenium data
text: Next, apply SCOPE B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} triad using default configurations following parameters: min.prop: minimum neighborhood cell-type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0 (default: 0.3). mgcv_df: dimension basis used thin-plate regression spline smoothing core cell-type proportions (default: 15; see mgcv::s() details). set number clusters clusternum 3 separate B cell T cell zones. can see B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell\mbox{B cell/CD4}^+\mbox{ T cell/CD8}^+\mbox{ T cell} CTNs closely aligns lymphocyte aggregates CRC samples, CTN1 resembles B cell zone CTN2 resembles surrounding T cell zone.   can compute evaluation metrics comparing CTN1 ground truth TLS annotations
code: core_celltypes <- c("B cells", "CD4+ T-cells", "CD8+ T-cells")  CTN_df <- run_SCOPE(   combination_table = as.data.frame(matrix(core_celltypes, nrow = 1)),   cell_neighbor_table = cell_neighbor_table_maxdist,   dat_in = PF_Xenium_dat_in,   save_results = FALSE,    clusternum = 3, min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE, mgcv_df = 15, iter.max = 100) # save(CTN_df, file = paste0(data_path, dataset_name, "_B_CD4T_CD8T.RData")) # Number of cells per CTN table(CTN_df$label) #>  #>       CTN1       CTN2 Unassigned  #>      42777      44617    1541512 # CTN label with colored dots separating the cell types CTN_label <- color_CTN_names(   paste(core_celltypes, collapse = "_"), PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)   # Visualizing B cell/CD4+ T cell/CD8+ T cell CTN CTN_df %>% guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3), ncol = 1)) +   facet_wrap(~ImageID, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +   theme_void(base_size = 14) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("CTN1" = "#E69F00", "CTN2" = "#56B4E9",                                  "Unassigned" = "grey90"), name = NULL) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"), TLS_img_list) %>%   filter(CellID %in% CTN_df$CellID) %>% # dropping cells with few neighbors   mutate(Celltype = ifelse(Celltype %in% core_celltypes, as.character(Celltype), "Other")) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = X, y = Y, color = Celltype)) +   geom_point(shape = 16, size= 0.1) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette, "Other" = "grey90")) +   guides(color = coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(2.5, "mm"),         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         panel.background = element_blank()) CTN_df %>%   left_join(PF_Xenium_dat_in, by = "CellID") %>%   count(Celltype, label) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = n, y = label, fill = factor(Celltype, names(PF_Xenium_celltype_palette)))) +   geom_col(position = position_fill(reverse = TRUE)) +   scale_fill_manual(values = PF_Xenium_celltype_palette, name = NULL,                     labels = rename_celltype) +   labs(title = CTN_label) +   scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE, clip = "off") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(ncol = 4)) +   theme_cowplot(font_size = 12) +   labs(x = "Proportion of Cells", y = legend.location = "plot",         legend.justification = "right") library(aricode); library(caret) # for clustering evaluation  cluster_eval_metrics <- CTN_df %>%    mutate(label_binary = ifelse(label Can used examine distribution distances. neighbor.cell.ids data frame containing cell ids nearest neighbors focal cell.

title: Constructing neighborhood cell type count and proportion matrices — Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: interact_matrix Input dat_in Input data frame \(N\) rows. Columns must contain CellID Celltype.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: \(T\times T\) matrix containing CLQs every cell type pair, \(T\) number available cell types. rows matrix focal cell types columns neighboring cell types.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ
text: colocalization pair cell types \(\) \(b\) tissue section \(l\) can evaluated CLQ metric $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow } = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} \frac{C_{b\rightarrow } / N_a}{N_b / (N - 1)} & N_a > 5 \mbox{} \ N_b > 5 \\ 0, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\), \(N_a\), \(N_b\), \(N\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

Tissue sections fewer min.ncell cells excluded analysis. num_cores number cores use pbmcapply::pbmclapply(), .e. many child processes run simultaneously. Can set 1 Windows.

title: Computing observed colocation quotient (CLQ) — compute_CLQ_observed
text: colocalization pair cell types \(\) \(b\) tissue section \(l\) can evaluated observed CLQ $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs} = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0, & N_a^{(l)} \leq 5 \mbox{ } \ N_b^{(l)} \leq 5 \\ \frac{C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs} / N_a^{(l)}}{N_b^{(l)} / (N^{(l)} - 1)}, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), obs}\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\), \(N_a^{(l)}\), \(N_b^{(l)}\), \(N^{(l)}\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

text: cell_neighbor_ids Cell IDs nearest neighbors obtained using Build_cell_neighbor_maxdist(). dat_in Input data frame \(N\) rows. Columns must contain CellID, ImageID Celltype. min.ncell Minimum cell number threshold. Tissue sections fewer min.ncell cells excluded analysis. num_cores number cores use pbmcapply::pbmclapply(), .e. many child processes run simultaneously. title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: data frame \(T\times T\times L\times \mbox{iter.num}\) rows containing permutated CLQs focal (Celltype) neighboring cell types (Celltype_neighbor) tissue section (ImageID), \(T\) number available cell types \(L\) number tissue sections.

title: Computing colocation quotient (CLQ) on permutated data — compute_CLQ_permutated
text: tissue section, cell type labels randomly shuffled iter.num (default: 500) times generate permutated CLQ values $$CLQ_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k} = \left\{\begin{array}{ll} 0, & N_a^{(l)} \leq 5 \mbox{ } \ N_b^{(l)} \leq 5 \\ \frac{C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k} / N_a^{(l)}}{N_b^{(l)} / (N^{(l)} - 1)}, & \text{otherwise} \end{array}\right.$$ \(C_{b\rightarrow }^{(l), k}\) number cells cell type \(b\) within neighborhood cell type \(\) iteration \(k, k=\)1,2,...,iter.num, \(N_a^{(l)}\), \(N_b^{(l)}\), \(N^{(l)}\) number cells cell type \(\), \(b\) total number cells tissue section \(l\), respectively.

title: Robust multiple kernel K-means clustering on a multiview data — mkkcEst_linear
text: X_m_list list length \(P\) (default: 4) containing \(P\) kernel matrices. centers number clusters \(K\). iter.max maximum number iterations allowed. default 100. epsilon Convergence threshold. default \(10^{-4}\). theta Initial values kernel coefficients. default 1/P views. alpha Decay rate used computing exponential moving average \(\theta\). interation \(\scriptl\), \({\theta^{(\scriptl)}}^{EMA} = \alpha \theta^{(\scriptl)} + (1-\alpha) {\theta^{(\scriptl-1)}}^{EMA}, \alpha\[0, 1]\). default value 0.5.

title: Robust multiple kernel K-means clustering on a multiview data — mkkcEst_linear
text: list contains: cluster vector integers (1:K) indicating cluster point allocated. totss total sum squares. withinss Matrix within-cluster sum squares cluster, one row per view. withinsscluster Vector within-cluster sum squares, one component per cluster. withinssview Vector within-cluster sum squares, one component per view. tot.withinss Total within-cluster sum squares, .e. sum(withinsscluster). betweenssview Vector  -cluster sum squares, one component per view. tot.betweenss -cluster sum squares, .e. totss-tot.withinss. clustercount number clusters K. coefficients kernel coefficients H continuous clustering assignment size number points, one component per cluster. iter number iterations. MbatchKm_WCSS_per_cluster Within-cluster sum squares cluster recovering cluster labels final H using minibatch K-means. MbatchKm_centroids Final cluster centroids.

title: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
code: run_SCOPE(   combination_table,   cell_neighbor_table,   dat_in,   clusternum = 2,   min.prop = 0.3,   linear = FALSE,   mgcv_df = 15,   iter.max = 100,   epsilon = 1e-04,   alpha = 0.5,   num_cores = 1,   rename_CTN = TRUE,   save_results = TRUE,   output_dir = getwd(),   suffix = "" )

title: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
text: combination_table Data frame containing cell type combinations CTN screening. row combination. cell_neighbor_table \(N\times T\) neighborhood cell type proportion table. Column names must contain cell type labels combination_table. dat_in Input data frame \(N\) rows. Columns must contain CellID Celltype. clusternum number clusters \(K\). default value 2. min.prop Minimum neighborhood cell type proportion threshold. Proportions value set 0. default value 0.3. linear Whether run linear version SCOPE (.e. applying thin plate regression spline smoothing). Default FALSE. mgcv_df dimension basis used represent thin plate regression spline smooth term. default 15. See mgcv::s details. Disabled linear=TRUE. iter.max maximum number iterations allowed. default 100. epsilon Convergence threshold. default \(10^{-4}\). alpha Decay rate \(\alpha\) used computing exponential moving average weight vector \(\theta\). interation \(l\), \({\theta^{(l)}}^{EMA} = \alpha \theta^{(l)} + (1-\alpha) {\theta^{(l-1)}}^{EMA}, \alpha\[0, 1]\). default value 0.5. num_cores number cores use pbmcapply::pbmclapply, .e. many child processes run simultaneously. Can set 1 Windows. rename_CTN Whether rename niches based proportion core cell types. cluster highest proportion named CTN clusternum = 2 CTN1 otherwise. cluster lowest core cell type proportion renamed Unassigned. clusters named CTN2, ... accordingly clusternum > 2. save_results Whether save niche screening results output directory. TRUE (default) results CTN saved separate file. FALSE CTN screening results combined single data frame returned function output. output_dir Output directory. automatically create new directory target directory exist. suffix File name suffix.

title: Multi-view cell type triad niche screening — run_SCOPE
text: res CTN screening results. returns save_results FALSE.
